feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Error 404 when trying to access a full stack CAP application deployed in Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have created a full stack CAP application and deployed it in Cloud Foundry. However when trying to access the app via HTML5 repository, I get a blank page and when I open the console, I see a bunch of 404 errors.

 

I have followed a youtube tutorial: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z10xke7yli4&list=PL54JdsBCvI0mPwzMkzlNnulw3NaxoWzks&index=23
The steps which I followed were exactly the same, still I am getting this issue.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Clone the github repository: https://github.com/developedbysom/cap-animal-adopt.git

2. Run npm install.

3. Run cds add hana --for production

4. Run cds add xsuaa --for production

5. Run cds add workzone (Since we are using managed approuter, this will also create xs-app.json in app/adopter)
6. Run npm install --prefix app/adopter/

7. Run cds add mta 
8. Run mbt build -t gen --mtar mta.tar && cf deploy gen/mta.tar

I have added my xs-app.json,mta.yaml and manifest.json files as txt in the question.
Please look into this and guide me if I am doing anything wrong.

Thanks,

Regards,

Raihan

entry.title: Error fiori app generator failed
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to generate a SAP Fiori generator in SAP business application studio but when choose template and press Next 

It have message error:

Help me to fix this

entry.title: Credit Limit Error When Creating Sales Order for Extended Sales Org
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a scenario related to SAP Credit Management and would appreciate your guidance.

I have a customer initially created under Sales Organization 2000 with Credit Segment 2000. I assigned a credit limit of 1000, and everything works fine — for example:

  • If I create a sales order of 900, it's saved successfully.

  • If I try to create an order of 1100, the system blocks it due to exceeding the credit limit, which is expected and working as intended.

Now, I extended the same customer to another Sales Organization 4000, and assigned Credit Segment 4000 with a credit limit of 5000.

However, when I try to create a sales order of 1600 in Sales Org 4000, the system throws an error saying that the credit limit is exceeded — even though the credit limit under Credit Segment 4000 is set to 5000.

Can anyone help me understand why this is happening? Am I missing some configuration or master data dependency that is causing the system to still consider the old credit segment or combine limits?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Faraz Ali.

entry.title: RAG Quickstart Sample app using CAP LLM Plugin: Error while generating and storing vector embeddings
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I'm encountering an issue while setting up my SAP RAG Quickstart sample application for hybrid testing. I've configured the destination service (rag-quick-start-app-destination-service) and my server module (rag-quick-start-app-srv) in my MTA deployment descriptor.

rag-quickstart-app 

I've ensured the destination binding is correctly configured, leveraging the service key information of my AI Core instance. My cds configuration in package.json is also set up to use this destination (named aicore-destination for the GenAI Hub).

However, during local testing (hybrid scenario), my application seems unable to read or resolve the destination information.

***********************************************************************************************

Received the request to split the document test.pdf and store it into SAP HANA Cloud!

Temporary PDF File restored and saved to: /home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/srv/test.pdf
Splitting the document into text chunks.
Documents split into 2 chunks.
Leveraging the CAP LLM plugin to generate the vector embeddings for the text chunks.

Error while generating and storing vector embeddings: Error: No configuration found for 'cds.requires.destination'
    at _required (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/factory.js:34:80)
    at new ServiceFactory (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/factory.js:20:16)
    at connect.to (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/cds-connect.js:48:17)
    at async CAPLLMPlugin.getEmbeddingWithConfig (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/cap-llm-plugin/srv/cap-llm-plugin.js:153:27)
    at async ApplicationService.<anonymous> (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/srv/embedding-storage.js:129:33)
    at async next (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:68:17)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:66:10)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/common/Service.js:106:16)
    at async /home/user/projects/rag-quickstart-app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/cds-services/adapter/odata-v4/handlers/action.js:53:16

 

could anyone provide insights or suggestions on how to further troubleshoot this issue?

entry.title: Build process automation subscription failed in trial account
entry.content:
Unable to add the SAP Build process automation to my sub account instance, even though i added SAP BUILD PROCESS AUTOMATION Entitlements to my Subaccount. I tried to add many times by deleting sub account and again creating subaccount, but the same problem exists here.

The following is the details of my US account.

It is giving the response, couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

entry.title: Unable to subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Team,   

I have a trail account in SAP BTP and unable to subscribe to build process automation with the free plan. Please help on this.

I'm currently facing issues creating a subscription for SAP Build Process Automation; it's giving me an error message saying the below message.

"Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.”
I have attached a screenshot of the error for your reference.

Please, could you assist me in resolving this issue? I appreciate your help in advance!

Best regards,

entry.title: List Control “Entity Set” pop up button missing
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to create an SAP Fiori App using SAP Business Application Studio, but "Select Entity Set" pop up missing. 

 

Regards!

Dan

entry.title: Error activating "SAP Build Process Automation" on a Trial account
Error : Executing CAP with SAP HANA Cloud project as MTA
entry.content:
Hello,

I deployed this project in my BTP trial version, "Combine CAP with SAP HANA Cloud to Create Full-Stack Applications".

It looks like it is correct the deployment, no errors I saw, however I get this message when I try to access the router url 

Cannot GET /

What looks like no to be correct is xsuaa instance, it was created with two binding but no key. -- see attachment.

Any idea is welcome, I ran out of ideas.

entry.title: SAP Build process automation subscription failed in trial account
entry.content:
Hi,

Subscription to Build process automation is failing in the trial account.

Region : US East (VA) - AWS.

Any leads are appreciated, thank you !

entry.title: SAP Datasphere and SAP HANA Cloud — Technical Activities During Table/View Creation, Persistence
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I would appreciate your guidance on the following questions related to SAP Datasphere and SAP HANA Cloud:

When we create a table in SAP Datasphere:

  • What operations are performed on the underlying SAP HANA Cloud database?

  • Is a physical table immediately created in HANA Cloud?

  • Are there any additional metadata artifacts generated at this stage?

When we create a view in SAP Datasphere:

  • How is this view represented on the HANA Cloud database side (e.g., SQL view, calculation view)?

  • Is any temporary or runtime structure created immediately?

When we persist data:

  • Into a table:
    What activities occur in HANA Cloud during data persistence into an existing Datasphere table?

  • Into a view (after enabling persistence):
    How is the persisted data stored in the underlying database?
    Is a new table automatically created? If yes, what is its structure relative to the original view definition?

 Regarding Partitioning and Data Loading:

  • When partitions are created on a Datasphere table or persisted view:

    • What database-level changes happen on SAP HANA Cloud?

  • When we load data into these partitions:

    • Are partitions managed internally as standard HANA partitions, or handled separately?

  • When we delete specific partitions or delete all partitions:

    • What database activities happen during this operation?

General Runtime Activities:

  • During all these operations (table/view creation, persistence, partitioning, deletion), what are the typical HANA Cloud runtime actions (e.g., commits, locks, internal optimizations, delta merges)?

Any insights, technical explanations, or references to SAP Datasphere documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Cannot connect to a service - Build code to SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello, I am developing an extension in SAP Build Code, and when trying to add a SSFF service, it does not appear as available.

 

 

The BTP Trial account has already been registered in the SSFF product, and the destination has been created.

 

 

As mentioned in the help documentation, it seems that this tool is not available when using the Full-Stack Application with the Productivity Tools dev space.

 

 

I would like to know if there is another way to add a SuccessFactors service in SAP Build Code within a Full-Stack Application project using the Productivity Tools dev space.

Has anyone else experienced the same issue?
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event based processes
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm currently following a codejam blog regarding event-based processes for sap build. here is the link of my current lesson: 3 - Explore SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh | SAP Tutorials

one of the prerequisite is a "participants handbook" which contains the system and credentials needed to follow the blog.

where can i get this? thanks

entry.title: Error in establishing IDoc connection between SAP CPI and S/4HANA
entry.content:
We are currently trying to establish a connection between SAP CPI and S/4HANA using the IDoc adapter. Our system currently only uses OData, and we wanted to explore more options in connectivity.

To start our project, we are trying to send a sales order from SAP CPI to S4. The IDoc is ORDERS05, in XML format and the structure is correct. See below:

<ORDERS05>
	<IDOC BEGIN="1">
		<EDI_DC40 SEGMENT="1">
			<TABNAM>EDI_DC40</TABNAM>
			<MANDT>120</MANDT>
			<DOCNUM></DOCNUM>
			<DOCREL>755</DOCREL>
			<STATUS>53</STATUS>
			<DIRECT>2</DIRECT>
			<OUTMOD/>
			<IDOCTYP>ORDERS05</IDOCTYP>
			<MESTYP>ORDERS</MESTYP>
			<SNDPOR>CPIDEV</SNDPOR>
			<SNDPRT>KU</SNDPRT>
			<SNDPRN>C000003</SNDPRN>
			<RCVPOR>SAPDS4</RCVPOR>
			<RCVPRT>KU</RCVPRT>
			<RCVPRN>C000003</RCVPRN>
			<CREDAT>20250402</CREDAT>
			<CRETIM>090303</CRETIM>
		</EDI_DC40>
		<E1EDK01>
			<ACTION>00</ACTION>
			<BSART>DS</BSART>
			<BELNR>30114186</BELNR>
		</E1EDK01>
		<E1EDK14>
			<QUALF>019</QUALF>
			<ORGID>ZEDI</ORGID>
		</E1EDK14>
		<E1EDK03>
			<IDDAT>010</IDDAT>
			<DATUM>20250402</DATUM>
		</E1EDK03>
		<E1EDK03>
			<IDDAT>002</IDDAT>
			<DATUM>20250403</DATUM>
		</E1EDK03>
		<E1EDKA1>
			<PARVW>WE</PARVW>
			<NAME1>MXXX KXXX</NAME1>
			<STRAS>000 RXXXX</STRAS>
			<ORT01>FXXXXX LXXXX</ORT01>
			<PSTLZ>00000</PSTLZ>
			<TELF1>000-000-0000</TELF1>
			<REGIO>NJ</REGIO>
		</E1EDKA1>
		<E1EDKA1>
			<PARVW>AG</PARVW>
			<LIFNR>93906NPAW</LIFNR>
		</E1EDKA1>
		<E1EDK02>
			<QUALF>001</QUALF>
			<BELNR>30114186</BELNR>
			<DATUM>20250402</DATUM>
		</E1EDK02>
		<E1EDK17>
			<QUALF>Z01</QUALF>
			<LKOND>CG</LKOND>
		</E1EDK17>
		<E1EDKT1>
			<TDID>ZEDH</TDID>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>IA|93906</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>SL|CG</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>VN|93906</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>ST|NPAW</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>BT|NPAW</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
		</E1EDKT1>
		<E1EDKT1>
			<TDID>ZSCH</TDID>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>:C000::003</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
		</E1EDKT1>
		<E1EDP01>
			<POSEX>1</POSEX>
			<MENGE>2</MENGE>
			<MENEE>EA</MENEE>
			<VPREI>112.2</VPREI>
			<E1EDP19>
				<QUALF>003</QUALF>
				<IDTNR>806134977252</IDTNR>
			</E1EDP19>
			<E1EDP19>
				<QUALF>001</QUALF>
				<IDTNR>4645188</IDTNR>
			</E1EDP19>
			<E1EDP19>
				<QUALF>002</QUALF>
				<IDTNR>H981101-AGB</IDTNR>
			</E1EDP19>
		</E1EDP01>
		<E1EDS01>
			<SUMID>001</SUMID>
			<SUMME>1</SUMME>
			<SUNIT>EA</SUNIT>
		</E1EDS01>
	</IDOC>
</ORDERS05>

Before trying to send this file, we have done the basics such as activate IDoc via SICF, creating an RFC connection in SM59, registering the IDoc service in SRTIDoc, we added the IDoc URL resource path (sap/bc/srt/idoc) in SAP Cloud Connector. We also created a new port via WE21. We followed this blog: SAP CPI to SAP ERP and SAP ERP to SAP CPI and did every step.

However, when we tried to send the file, we got this error in SAP CPI Monitoring:

I checked error logs via SRT_ELOG in S4, and got the same error:

We cannot find any blog or SAP note that could help us. Is there anyone who experienced this and how were you able to fix it?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite helps you to quickly develop and manage reliable communication between applica
How to use nested $filter inside $expand while working with projection functions, CQL, OData?
entry.content:
Hello team,
I have following Odata query which works fine in Postman. Now I have to implement same logic in CAP.
.com/odata/v4/OPPS/BusinessUnitAssignments?$filter=(businessUnitID eq '1234')&$expand=promotion($filter=statusCode eq 'AC' and filterBuId eq 'CZP1%7C2B';$expand=promotionPriceDerivationRules($select=priceDerivationRuleID)) 

Here we need to expand promotion but only include promotions that match statusCode='AC' and filterBuId='CZP1|2B'

As you can see in query, we have to use 2 times expand, hence joined query with "semicolon". But in first expand we must use $filter, but in the beginning of query, we have already used $filter.
In CAP as we know, $filter == where()

Are there any limitations in .column() projection while doing filtering inside expansions?
How to use .where() clause in this situation?
Please support. Thanks.
Ex- This below snippet examples works fine but now how to use statusCode and filterBuId

            const results = await remote.run(
                SELECT.from(BusinessUnitAssignments)
                    .columns(b => {
                        b.businessUnitID,
                        b.promotion(p => {
                            p.promotionID,
                            p.promotionPriceDerivationRules(r => {
                                r.promotionPriceDerivationRuleID
                            })
                        })
                    })
                    .where({
                        businessUnitID: CustomerID,
                    })
            );

@vvdries @gregorw @qmacro

entry.title: SAP CAP - How to Set Attribute Type as Integer When Mocking User in package.json?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently mocking a user in my package.json for local testing in a CAP project, like this:

 

"gabriel": {
  "password": "@Teste123",
  "roles": ["AMS"],
  "attr": {
    "ams": "1"
  }
}

In the restrict rule, I need to compare the ams attribute as an integer:

{
  grant: ['READ'],
  to   : ['FORNECEDOR', 'TRANSPORTADOR', 'AMS'],
  where: '( CriadoPor = $user or Transportadora = $user ) or $user.AMS = 1'
}

This works fine in the deployed environment, where xs-security.json defines the ams attribute like this:

 
"attributes": [
  {
    "name": "ams",
    "description": "ams",
    "valueType": "int"
  }
]

However, during local development, the mocked ams attribute is always treated as a string (e.g., "1"), and the comparison with the integer 1 in the where clause fails.

Is there a way to define the type of a mocked attribute as int in package.json, or any workaround to handle this locally?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP BTP iOS Trial error - 404 in WebViewAuthenticator and no logs
entry.content:
I try to set up a native iOS app with the SAP BTP iOS SDK from scratch.
(I followed the guidelines and configured both the Mobile Services Cockpit and the app.)
When I try to log in with the app, during the onboarding, I get a 404 error in the WebViewAuthenticator after entering and sending the user password. Unfortunately there is no logging during the WebView, so I have no idea what's wrong or going on.
Any idea what can be the problem or how to solve or get some log entries?

entry.title: Desktop Agent Not Receiving Project in Deployed Mode
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m following the "Process and approve your invoices with SAP Build Process Automation" mission from SAP Discovery Center.

Link: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/145344/cardName/Invoice%20automatically%20processed%20and%20posted%20to%20SAP%20S%2F4HANA%20Cloud/?tab=projectboard

I am currently facing an issue with the SAP Build Process Automation Desktop Agent. The agent is running fine in editable/test mode and the automation works as expected.

However, once I deploy the project, and trigger the process via form link, the Desktop Agent does not receive the project.

The following actions have already been checked/completed:

* Agent is properly configured and in attended mode.

* Required attributes have been added to the agent and to the project.

* Connection between Desktop Agent & Tenant : Active

* The agent is assigned to the correct environment.

* Tried restarting the agent and redeploying your project.

Despite all these steps, the Desktop Agent still displays the message: "It looks like no project has been received yet. When there are, you'll see them here."

In SAP Community tab, I've seen a post related to my issue and they said "You need to create an Automation Launcher in able for your automation to be called on the machine's desktop agent.

Your project needs to be added in "Automation Launchers" to be distributed to Attended Agents:"

In my project, already automation launcher is there. But I can't able to add my project under "Automation Launchers" tab.

Can someone please help identify what might be missing or misconfigured? Any guidance or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD calculate tax issue
entry.content:
Spoiler
 

Hello,

 

I want to calculate tax using 'CALCULATE_TAX_FROM_NET_AMOUNT' function but it's not permitted in the public cloud. 

What am I supposed to use instead?

Thanks.
